title: ～どころではない
titleEn: Not the time/place for; far from; can't possibly.
structure: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない
structureEn: Verb-casual + どころではない, い-Adjective + どころではない, な-Adjective + どころではない, Noun + どころではない
explanation: Not the time/place for; far from; can't possibly. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～どころではない grammar point is used to express that circumstances make it impossible or inappropriate to do something—there’s a much bigger issue at hand. It can also mean ‘far from’ in a figurative sense.

title: ～としたら
structure: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら
structureEn: Verb-casual + としたら, い-Adjective + としたら, な-Adjective + だとしたら, Noun + だとしたら
explanation: Express a hypothetical situation; 'if', 'suppose', 'assuming'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～としたら grammar point is used to express a hypothetical situation or assumption. It can be translated as 'if', 'suppose', or 'assuming' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it’s used with verbs, adjectives, or nouns.

title: ～としても
structure: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても
structureEn: Verb-casual + としても, い-Adjective + としても, な-Adjective + だとしても, Noun + だとしても
explanation: Even if, despite, although (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～としても grammar point is used to say ‘even if...’ or ‘even assuming that...’, often followed by a conclusion or stance. It’s similar to ～ても, but puts more emphasis on the hypothetical assumption or viewpoint.

title: ～と～ともに
titleEn: Expresses 'together with' or 'along with'; used to describe two events or actions happening simultaneously or in parallel.
structure: Verb-casual + とともに, い-Adjective + とともに, な-Adjective + とともに, Noun + とともに
structureEn: Verb-casual + とともに, い-Adjective + とともに, な-Adjective + とともに, Noun + とともに
explanation: Expresses 'together with' or 'along with'; used to describe two events or actions happening simultaneously or in parallel. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～と～ともに grammar point is used to express that two events or actions are happening simultaneously or in parallel. It is translated as 'together with' or 'along with' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

title: ～とは限らない
titleEn: Expresses that something is not necessarily true; 'not always', 'not necessarily'.
structure: Verb-casual + とは限らない, い-Adjective + とは限らない, な-Adjective + だとは限らない, Noun + だとは限らない
structureEn: Verb-casual + とは限らない, い-Adjective + とは限らない, な-Adjective + だとは限らない, Noun + だとは限らない
explanation: Expresses that something is not necessarily true; 'not always', 'not necessarily'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とは限らない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily true or does not always occur. It can be translated as 'not always' or 'not necessarily' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

title: ～ないことはない
titleEn: Express that something is not impossible, meaning it is possible or could be done.
explanation: Express that something is not impossible, meaning it is possible or could be done. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ないことはない grammar point is used to express a double negative, meaning that something is not impossible, implying that it is possible or could be done. Essentially, it downplays the possibility or ability to do something but still acknowledges that it's possible.

title: ～ないこともない
titleEn: Used to express an action or state is 'not impossible', but emphasizes reluctance or doubt.
structure: Verb-ないForm + こともない, い-adjective + こともない
structureEn: Verb-ないForm + こともない, い-adjective + こともない
explanation: Used to express an action or state is 'not impossible', but emphasizes reluctance or doubt. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ないこともない grammar point is used to express that something is not impossible, but the speaker is reluctant, has doubts, or deems it as a less favorable option. It can be translated as 'not that one can't', 'not that it's impossible', or 'it's not out of the question'.

title: ～ないではいられない
titleEn: Cannot help but; cannot avoid; unable to refrain from.
structure: Verb-ない form + ではいられない or じゃいられない
structureEn: Verb-ない form + ではいられない or じゃいられない
explanation: Cannot help but; cannot avoid; unable to refrain from. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ないではいられない grammar point is used to express a strong feeling, urge, or uncontrollable action that someone cannot resist or help doing, even if it may be undesirable. It can be translated as 'cannot help but', 'cannot avoid', or 'unable to refrain from' in English.
